def: adv. By degrees, gradually (syn. rafta-rafta).
s.f. = next, q.v.

ety: v.n. from baṭā-nā; or = S. वण्ट+यं
def: s.f. Division, partition; part, share; share of produce; division of the crop; apportionment in kind; rent of land paid in kind; — division of the crop between the cultivator and zamīndār (or the Government, as landlord)
subs: s.f. Division of produce or crops
s.m. A cultivator who is a shareholder with the landlord in the crop.

def: s.f. = baṭwāʼī, q.v.
s.f. The art of twisting silver thread (or gold thread) and silk together (to form kalā-battūn).
s.f. = baṭairī, q.v.
s.m. Weights and scales. (See bāṭ.)
s.f. A Hindū marriage ceremony in which the bride presents the bridegroom with a wedding garment and some money.
s.f. A Hindū marriage ceremony in which the bride presents the bridegroom with a wedding garment and some money.
s.m. = baṭwaiyā, baṅṭait, q.q.v.
s.m. One who twists silver (or gold) thread and silk together; a maker of kalā-battūn.
s.f. Duck = bat̤ak, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Garrulous, loquacious; — great talker, &c. (syn. bat-kahā, bātūnī).
s.f. Folly, vanity; falseness, falsehood.
s.m. = bathān, baṭhān, q.q.v.

ety: batā˚ = batāw˚ = Prk. वत्तावे(इ) = S. वर्त्त (rt. वृत्)+ आपय(ति)
def: s.m. A bracelet or ring of metal (worn by men and women); — an under-turban (used to swell out and-give shape to the outer turban, or to keep it from being soiled by the perspiration of the head); an old or ragged turban; — livelihood, maintenance, support, help, aid.
wety: From Sanskrit वत् (vat) + ـآ (-ā, causative augment) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix).

ety: S. वार्त्त, rt. वृत्, or rt. वत्+H. caus. aff. ānā
def: v.t. To cause to comprehend or understand, to teach, to make intelligible, to explain, describe, shew, point out, direct, indicate, signify; to tell, say, relate, make known, discover, disclose; to indicate or make known by signs, gestures, &c.; to gesticulate; to instruct as to, to appoint (a task, work, &c.).; to teach (one) a lesson, to chastise; — v.n. To seem to be, to appear
wety: From Sanskrit वत् (vat) + ـآ (-ā, causative augment) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix).

def: v.t. To cause to be shared or divided; to divide or divert (the attention) = baṭwānā.
v.t. To cause to be twisted ( = baṭwānā).
(prop. bit̤āna; for A. بطانة), s.m. lit. "Lining'; cap or cloth worn under the turban (syn. tah-peć).
adj. In straits, in difficulties; distressed, harassed, vexed
subs: ba-tang honā, v.n. To be distressed, &c.

def: s.m. Proprietor or holder of a share (baṭ or baṅṭ), shareholder.
s.m. (from next) = batāʼī, q.v.
s.m. Divisibility.
s.m.? Land in a state fit for the plough.
adj. Dock-tailed (animal), wanting the tail; mutilated; — destitute of good qualities ( = abtar, q.v.).
s.m. Name of a tribe of Gūjars, q.v.
adj. Worse; very bad.
adv. Aside, apart ( = bar t̤araf).
adv. By the way; by way (of), by means (of).
adv. In the mode or manner (of); by way (of).
s.f. Wind, air
subs: s.f. A kind of sweetmeat (cf. next).

def: v.n. To dissolve or melt away like a batāsā, to fade rapidly away; to become a shadow.
v.n. To dissolve or melt away like a batāsā, to fade rapidly away; to become a shadow.
adj. (f. -ī), Indicating, indicatory, representing, &c.
subs: s.f. (Math.) Line of representation or demarcation; — batātī lakīr nāp, s.f. Analytical Geometry (syn. bīj-gaṅit nāp; ḵẖarāsh-ë-maḵẖrūt̤).

ety: S. वर्त, or H. बाट 'road'+S. aff. आकु
def: s.m. Wayfarer, traveller; highway robber (?).
adv. After the manner (of), like; in a manner; by way (of)
subs: adv. In a manner peculiar to himself (or themselves); in his (or their) own way; on his (or their) own account; of himself; of themselves.

def: s.f. A flatulent or œdematous swelling; tumour; boil (syn. phuṛiyā, bal-toṛ).
v.t. = batānā, q.v.
s.m. The shaddock or pumelo, Citrus decumana (syn. ćakotrā).
v.t. = baṭānā, baṭwānā, q.v.
s.m. Holder of a share, &c. = baṭaniyā.
s.f. The quail, Perdix olivacea.
s.m. Way, road; path, pathway, passage (syn. bāṭ and baṭiyā).
s.m. Hut or shed in the jungle (used by graziers); cattle-fold, open place in a field into which cattle are driven at night; pasture-ground (syn. baṭhān).
s.m. = bathān, q.v.
s.f. Heap of dried cow-dung.
v.n. To enter ( = paiṭhnā, baiṭhnā, q.v.).
v.n. To be twisted, &c. = baṭnā, q.v.
v.t. To share, &c. = bāṅṭnā, q.v.
s.m. = bathuʼā, q.v.
s.m. The pot-herb Chenopodium album; — orach; the herb 'all-heal'; a herb which springs up with the rabīʻ or spring crops.
s.f. Wick of a lamp, &c. = battī, q.v.
s.f. Cake of bread baked upon cinders.
s.f. Site of a building, place; house, dwelling, habitation, home; garden-house, villa; garden, enclosure, &c. ( = bāṛī, q.v.); the groin; rod, mace.
s.f. Word, speech (cf. bati-yānā).
adv. Somewhere or other ( = bāṭ-ghāṭ).
s.f. Villa, &c. = bāṭī and bāṛī, q.q.v.

ety: act. part. of بطل, 'to be false,' &c.
def: adj. False, untrue, wrong, incorrect; fictitious, spurious, unreal, unfounded, unsound; vain, futile, worthless, useless, unprofitable; devoid of virtue or efficacy, of no effect, ineffectual, null, void, of no force, of no account, naught, going for nothing; annulled, abolished, counteracted
subs: s.m. Counteractor or annuller of enchantment; that which or he who frustrates incantation
v.t. To regard, or treat, as false, &c.; to treat as a nullity; to set at naught

v.t. To falsify; to prove to be false; to render ineffectual, vain, useless, &c.; to nullify, vitiate, invalidate, make void; to annul, abolish; to set aside, supersede; to frustrate, counteract, thwart
v.t. To consider false, vain, &c. = bāt̤il samajhnā, q.v.
v.n. To be or prove vain, ineffectual, &c.; to come to naught; to become void; to be nullified, frustrated, &c.; to be annulled or abolished.

def: s.f. Vanity, folly, absurdity.

ety: act. part. of بطن, 'to penetrate or enter into'
def: adj. & s.m. Unapparent, hidden, concealed, covert, inward, inner, interior, internal, intrinsic, esoteric (opp. of z̤āhir); — the internal or intrinsic state, character or circumstances (of a man or a thing); the inward part; the secret thoughts; mind, heart; disposition of the mind; recesses of the mind.
adj. = bāt̤in, q.v.
s.f. A species of egg-plant; a kind of brinjal; green or unripe fruit or vegetable (generally); — adj. Unripe, raw, green; soft, tender, delicate ( = komal).
s.f. (dim. of bāṭ, 'road'), Path, footpath, pathway, narrow passage.
s.f. A small weight (bāṭ); a small round stone for weighing, grinding, &c.; — a cocoanut; (met.) the breasts (of a woman).
s.f. Talking; talk, conversation, &c. = bāt-ćīt.
v.n. To talk, speak, converse, discourse ( = bāt karnā, batrānā).
adj. & s.m. See s.v. bat.
s.m. Weight used in weighing goods.
s.f. Powder-flask.
s.m. Rendering vain, rendering abortive or ineffectual.
adj. (prov.) = baṭurā, 'assembled.'
s.f. Vanity; falseness; abortion; the becoming of no effect.
v.t. To explain, tell, &c. = batānā, q.v.; — v.n. To talk, converse, &c. = bitrānā, batiyānā, q.q.v.
s.f. A brass vessel from which Hindūs drink water, and in which they cook their food (syn. loṭā).
s.f. A brass vessel from which Hindūs drink water, and in which they cook their food (syn. loṭā).
s.m. See s.v. baṭ, 'road.'
s.m. lit. 'The twining or climbing jasmine'; the double jasmine, Jasminum zambac (syn. belā).
s.m. The belly, abdomen; the womb (syn. peṭ.)
subs: adv. Generation after generation ( = pusht dar pusht).

ety: bat˚ = Prk. वट्ट(इ) = S. वट्य(ते), pass. of rt. वट्
def: v.n. To be divided, shared, or distributed (cf. baṅṭnā, bāṅṭnā); — v.t. To divide, share, &c.; to obtain, gain, make profit (e.g. ham-ne āj ṣubḥ-se das rupaya baṭā)
v.t. To twine, twist, form by convolutions (as thread, rope, &c.), to plait, weave; — v.n. To be twisted, &c.
s.m. An instrument with which ropes are twisted.
v.t. To share, &c. = bāṅṭnā, q.v.
adj. Of the belly; of the womb, born of.
s.m. Wayfarer, traveller ( = baṭāʼū, q.v. — The form baṭoʼiyā also occurs).
s.m. Wayfarer, traveller ( = baṭāʼū, q.v. — The form baṭoʼiyā also occurs).
s.m. Talking (one) into, duping, overreaching, &c. ( = buttā, q.v.); — deceiver, cajoler, cheat
subs: (-ke), To be wheedled, duped, deceived, cheated, &c. ( = dam-meṅ ānā).

def: s.m. = baṭoran, q.v.
s.m. = baṭoran, q.v.
s.f. Loquacious woman, one who talks people over; a wheedling, deceitful woman; a cheat.
s.f. Vain or frivolous talk; jesting, buffoonery.
s.m.? Cool refreshing wind that blows down from the snows of the Himalayas after sunset. (The term is used chiefly by the hill-people: in the plains this wind is called ḍhāḍhū.)
s.m. Gathering, assemblage, crowd; resort.
s.m. = baṭoran, q.v.
s.m. Gathering in or collecting grain at the time of harvest; pickings, gleanings; sweepings, rubbish.
v.t. To gather up, collect, purse; to bring together, assemble; to accumulate, amass.
s.m. See s.v. baṭ, 'road.'
v.n. To talk, converse = batiyānā, batlānā, q.q.v.

ety: dim. of baṭṭā, or = S. वटक+र+इका
def: s.f. A small cup.
s.m. A kind of rice.
adj. Kind, loving, &c. = vatsal, q.v.

s.m. Exchange, discount; difference of exchange; deduction, deficiency, loss; defect, blemish, flaw; fault, offence, injury; derogation, detraction; stain, stigma; — a cylinder of wood or stone with which condiments, drugs, &c. are frayed or pulverized (on a slab called sil, q.v.), a muller; — jewel-box, casket; betel-box (syn. ḍibiyā); a juggler's cup or box; a round looking-glass or mirror
subs: (-meṅ), Loss (of reputation, &c.) to accrue, stain to fall (upon); to be stained, sullied, &c. ( = baṭṭā lagnā)
v.t. To pay discount or exchange; to suffer a loss; to make up a loss or deficiency
adj. & s.m. Level, even; — levelling; ruins, complete destruction

(-ko, -meṅ), To charge discount; to cast a stain or slur (upon, -meṅ), derogate or detract (from); to defame, calumniate, vilify, stigmatize; — baṭṭā lagnā (-ko, -meṅ), To be liable or subject to discount; to suffer the casting of a stain or slur (on); to lose (one's) good name or reputation; to be sullied, tarnished, &c.
s.m. Juggler; trickster, humbug, cheat, swindler; — baṭṭe-bāzī, s.f. Juggling, jugglery, sleight of hand; trickery, cheating, swindling
adv. At a discount or advantage; at a reduction (e.g. baṭṭe-par ḵẖarīdnā)
adj. Liable to discount; injured, defaced, cracked (coin, &c.)
s.m. Loss and gain, profit and loss; bad debts; irrecoverable balance; — baṭṭe-khāte likhnā (-ko), To put down to profit and loss, enter as a doubtful debt
(-meṅ), To take or charge discount ( = baṭṭā lagānā).

def: s.m. = بٿا baṭṭā, q.v.; — and incorrectly) = bhatta, q.v.
s.f. = bat̤t̤aḵẖ, q.v.
s.f. Duck; duckling; a vessel formed in imitation of a duck.
wety: Hypercorrection of Classical Persian بطک (bat̤ak, “duck”), a wanderwort related to Arabic بَطَّة (baṭṭa), بَطّ (baṭṭ) (which itself is from Classical Persian بت (bat), perhaps with the diminutive suffix ـه (-a)), Old Armenian բադ (bad) and Sanskrit वर्तक (vartaka, “quail”), among others. Doublet of بَطّ (batt) and بدک (badak). Cognate with Punjabi ਬਤਕ (batak), ਬੱਤਖ਼ (battax) / بَطَّخ (bat̤t̤ax), Gujarati બતક (bata…

def: adj. Very foolish or vain; false.
s.m. See s.v. baṭṭā.

(-ko), To show a light; to apply a match (to); to set alight, to burn.

s.m. battīs-dhārā, s.f. Mother's milk, which is supposed to flow in thirty-two streams).

def: s.m. A tonic composed of thirty-two ingredients given to fever patients, to women after childbirth, and to mares after foaling.

def: s.m. = baṭ-lohī, q.v.

s.m. Interior, inside; heart, mind; — adj. Concealed.

see s.v. bā.
s.m. Janitor, porter.
s.m. = bayā, q.v.
s.m. Sweetmeats, brass vessels, &c. presented to the bridegroom, &c. by the bride on the sankrāṅt of the month Māgh.
s.f. Age; time of life.
s.m. The weaver. bird, Ploceus baya, or Fringilla (Euplectes) Philippina.
s.m. An assizer ( = nirḵẖī); a person appointed in bazars to measure grain.
part. adj. (f. bāʼī), Opened, spread, stretched, &c.
adj. & adv. Apart, separate, distinct; another, quite another (e.g. yěh bāt bāyab hai).
s.m. The North-west.
s.m. Desert, wilderness
subs: bayābān-navard, adj. & s.m. Traversing deserts; — one who traverses the desert, wanderer

def: adj. Of or pertaining to the wilderness or desert; wild.
adv. In the hand, in hand.

r: bāyad, bāyistan, bāyad-shāyad, bāyad o shāyad
def: adj. As it should be, fit, proper.
s.f. Fee of a grain-measurer; weighman's perquisite.
adj. Windy, flatulent; indigestible
subs: s.m. The longing of a pregnant woman.

def: adj. (f. bāʼīṅ) & s.m. Left; — the left hand ( = bāṅwān, bāṅyāṅ); (in music) the bass; the bass sound (particularly of the t̤abla, ḍholak, and pakhāwaj, which are played with the left hand)
subs: v.n. To have an omen from the left-hand side (which is regarded as good)
(or pair) pūjnā (-kā), lit. 'To worship the left leg (of)'; to acknowledge the superiority or cleverness or cunning (of)
bāṅʼeṅ, bāṅweṅ, adv. To the left; on the left-hand side.

def: s.m. Presents of sweetmeats, &c., forming part of an offering to a deity, or prepared on festive occasions, such as marriages, &c., and sent to friends and acquaintances; — share, allotted portion.
s.m. = bayā; and adj. = bāyāṅ.
s.m. Declaration, assertion, affirmation; explanation, exposition, description, relation, disclosure, unfolding, circumstantial indication or evidence; perspicuity, clearness
subs: v.n. To contradict oneself; to prevaricate
v.n. To be affirmed, explained, &c.; to be clear, &c. ( = bayān honā)
v.t. To tell, relate, express, state, report; to declare, assert, affirm, depose; to describe, give an account of; to explain, to make clear or manifest
v.n. To be stated, affirmed, &c.; to be explained, unfolded, &c., to be made clear or manifest.

def: s.m. Earnest-money (corr. from بيعانه baiʻāna, q.v.).
s.f. Wind, air.
s.f. Wind, &c. = bayār, q.v.
s.f. Whiteness (contr. of sawād); blank book, commonplace book, note-book; account-book (syn. bahī); name of a figure in the science of ramal or geomancy.
s.m. Seller, merchant.

ety: perf. part. of bāyistan, 'to be necessary'
def: adj. Necessary, requisite; becoming, fit, proper.

ety: of bāḵẖtan; rt. Zend baz; S. भज्; cf. baḵẖt
def: s.m. Joker, jester; — kabūtar-bāz, s.m. 'Pigeon-fancier'; shatranj-bāz, 'chess-player.'

ety: old P. avāz, afāć; Zend apāś = S. अपाञ्च, fr. añć, with apa
def: adv. Back; again; — adj. Thrown back; open
subs: (-se), To come or turn back (from), draw back (from); to leave off, desist, refrain, abstain (from); to give up, abandon, relinquish, renounce; to keep (from), avoid, shun; to decline, refuse; — bāz ā, intj. Hold! stop! cease! have done! enough!

s.f. Inquiry, investigation, examination; minute investigation; accountableness, responsibility; — bāz-purs karnā, To inquire (into); to examine; to call in question; to demand an explanation, call to account
(-se), To keep or hold (one) back (from); to keep back, detain, withhold; to restrain, repress, check; to prevent, prohibit, forbid; to exclude; to debar; to dissuade, deter
(-se), To keep (oneself) back (from), hold (oneself) aloof (from); to desist, cease (from); to abstain, refrain; to shun, avoid; to be prevented or prohibited (from)
s.f. Turning back, returning; return; retreat; — part. = next, q.v.
s.m. Getting back, recovering; resumption (of anything); the act of resuming alienated lands or property of any kind; — bāz-yāftagī, s.f. Idem.

def: s.m.f. Hawk; female falcon (the male is termed shāhīn)
subs: bāz-gīr, s.m. Falconer; — bāz-dārī, s.f. The keeping or charge of hawks or falcons; falconry.

def: adj. & s.m. Some, some few, certain, several; sundry, diverse, miscellaneous; some people; certain ones (baʻẓā is either the A. acc. of baʻẓ, or an Urdū formation therefrom).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَعْض (ba'z), from Arabic بَعْض (baʕḍ).

def: s.m. Sin, crime, guilt
adj. & s.m. Some, some few, certain, several; sundry, diverse, miscellaneous; some people; certain ones (baʻẓā is either the A. acc. of baʻẓ, or an Urdū formation therefrom).
adv. In appearance, apparently, outwardly, externally; ostensibly ( = ba-ṣūrat, q.v.).
s.m. An instrument for levelling ploughed land, a kind of harrow.
adj. After that, afterwards.
part. (of bāḵẖtan; bāz, q.v.+Zend añt = S. ant), Playing; player; — s.m. Tumbler pigeon.
corr. of bāzār, q.v.
s.m. Market, market-place, bazar, mart; business of a market; people assembled at a market; rate, price; demand, sale
subs: s.m. Discount; the market rate of exchange
s.f. Fee or tax for holding a stall or shop, or for trading in a market ( = tah-bāzārī)
bāzār ćamaknā, v.n. = bāzār garm honā, q.v.
bāzār-kā nirḵẖ, s.m. Market-rate; selling price
s.m. The custom or practice of the market
v.n. To market, to go to market

(-kā), The market to be brisk or active; to have a good sale; to be in great demand; to be actively carried on (a practice, e.g. rishwat-kā bāzār garm hai); to be all the rage; to rage, prevail (as an epidemic disease; e.g. haiẓe-kā bāzār garm hai)

v.n. To establish a market; to open a number of shops; to display (one's) wares; to be about one's business or trade; to have things spread out or scattered about in disorder; to make a noise and uproar (like that of a market)
(-kā), The market to be dull; to be in little demand
To go about (as if) measuring the bazar; to saunter about, roam, lounge.

def: s.m. Merchant, trader, dealer in the market.
s.f. Merchandize, traffic, trade, commerce.
adj. Of or appertaining to a market; market; ordinary, common; low, vulgar; — bāzārī, bāzārī ādmī, s.m. Common people
subs: bāzārī ḵẖabar, s.f. Bazar report, mere rumour
s.f. Market woman; — common woman, harlot.

def: adv. By way (of), by means (of), through; by reason of ( = ba-wasīla, q.v.).
adj. = bāzārī, q.v.
s.m. = pazāwa, pajāwa, q.q.v.
s.m. Mace, spice ( = basbāsa, q.v.).
adj. & s.m. Some, some few, certain, several; sundry, diverse, miscellaneous; some people; certain ones (baʻẓā is either the A. acc. of baʻẓ, or an Urdū formation therefrom).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَعْض (ba'z), from Arabic بَعْض (baʕḍ).

def: adj. Upside down, &c. = wāzh-gūn, q.v.
s.m. Tribute, &c. = bāj, q.v.
s.f. Play, sport, game, trick; game of chance, hazard; gaming; stake (at play), wager, bet
subs: bāzī lagānā, v.n. To lay a bet, to wager; to stake
bāzī jītnā, bāzī lenā, v.n. To win the game, or stakes; to be victorious, successful, or prosperous; to come off best, be first
To make game of, laugh at; to trick; to beat, defeat, check-mate
(-se), To play (with); to contend (against); to trifle (with), &c. = bāzī denā, q.v.
v.n. To be beaten, to lose, be cast
s.f. Place for exhibiting feats of activity, &c., theatre

s.m. (f. bāzī-garan and bāzī-garnī), One who exhibits feats of activity, legerdemain, &c.; tumbler, rope-dancer, juggler, conjurer; — bāzī-garī, s.f. The act of tumbling, &c.; rope-dancing, legerdemain, juggling, conjuring
(-se), To carry off the stakes (from), to win; to bear away the palm, to excel

def: s.f. Fun, play, sport; wagering; toy, plaything.
s.m. Giving liberally; munificence; gift, present; expense
s.m. Jest, bon-mot, &c. = baẕla, q.v.
s.m. Witticism, pleasantry, jest, joke, raillery (syn. lat̤īfa)
subs: adj. & s.m. Witty; — wit; jester, buffoon.

def: s.f. Assembly, company, party, entertainment, feast, banquet.
adv. By force, perforce, by main force, forcibly, on compulsion (syn. zabar-dastī, ba-jabr)
subs: v.t. To take by force; to extort, exact.

def: s.m. Seed-corn, seed
s.m. corr. of بذر baẕr, q.v.

ety: Pehl. bājāi; Zend, bāzu, S. बाहु
def: s.m. Ornament worn on the arm, armlet; bracelet
subs: (-ko), To lend a helping hand, to help, support, aid ( = bāṅh denā).
ex: اس کے بازو پر ایک نشان ہے۔
He has a scar on his arm.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَازُو (bāzū). Doublet of بَان٘ہہ (bā̃h).

def: s.m. Fine linen.
s.m. Cloth-merchant, draper, mercer.
s.m. Cloth-market.
s.m. Cloth-market.
s.f. Business of a cloth-merchant or mercer; drapery, linen-drapery, haberdashery.

adj. (f. -ī), Crooked, transverse, athwart, across ( = beṛhā, q.v.); awkward, untoward, unmanageable, difficult, hard; cross-grained, churlish, rude, rough, uncivilized, uncouth; — s.m. The (wooden) bolt or bar placed across a door to fasten it.
prep. (governing a following gen. with -ke); and prefix; — Without, devoid of; out of, &c.; in-, un-, im-, ir-, dis-, -less, &c.
subs: adj. Without water; without lustre, lustreless; without temper; without dignity, &c. (see āb)
s.f. Want of water; lustrelessness; want of temper, &c.
s.f. Want of agreement or harmony, discord
adj. Without impression or effect, ineffectual, inefficacious
s.f. Inefficacy: be-ajal, adj. Untimely (death); — be-ěḥtiyāt̤, adj. Incautious; wanting in carefulness; improvident; imprudent; rash
adj. & adv. Without choice, involuntary, constrained, forced, compelled; without self-possession, control, or authority; — involuntarily, against (one's) will, in spite of oneself, perforce
s.f. Without choice or election, helplessness, want of power
s.f. Non-performance, non-fulfilment; faithlessness; incivility, rudeness

adj. & s.m. Ill-behaved, unmannerly, disrespectful, rude; presumptuous, impudent, insolent; — an ill-behaved person, &c.: be-adabī, s.f. Incivility, rudeness, disrespect, impudence, impertinence, presumptuousness
(S. vyartha), adj. Without meaning, unmeaning, meaningless; useless, vain, unprofitable, ineffectual, inefficient
adj. Without goods or effects, having no effects
s.f. Want of goods, effects, or necessaries
adj. Without foundation, unfounded, groundless, baseless, false
adj. Of no credit or estimation, not to be trusted or relied on, untrustworthy, unreliable, faithless; incredible; unbelieving, incredulous, suspicious, sceptical
s.f. Want of credit, discredit, mistrust, distrust, disbelief; untrustworthiness, faithlessness, dishonesty
adj. Uneven, devoid of proportion or symmetry
adj. Regardless, inattentive; inconsiderate, unconcerned, unkind
s.f. Inattention, regardlessness, want of regard or kindness, disregard, inconsiderateness, unconcern
adj. Devoid of friendship or attachment; averse to friendship, &c., unsocial
s.f. The being devoid of friendship; want of affection or attachment; unsociableness
adj. & s.m. Undiscriminating, undistinguishing, indiscreet; ill-bred, rude, unmannerly, impertinent, presumptuous; — an indiscriminating person, &c.
s.f. Lack of discrimination; indiscretion; unmannerliness, incivility, rudeness, presumption
s.f. Disorder, misgovernment, mismanagement, &c. ( = bad-intiz̤amī, q.v.)
adj. Boundless, infinite, endless, &c. ( = be-andāza, be-ḥad)
adj. & adv. Thoughtless; fearless; thoughtlessly; fearlessly
adj. Unjust, unfair, inequitable; iniquitous, tyrannical, oppressive; unfeeling, hard-hearted, cruel (syn. anyāʼī)
adj. Without offspring or issue, childless
adj. Without religion, infidel; without conscience, unprincipled, corrupt, faithless, false, perfidious, treacherous; dishonest, fraudulent

s.f. Irreligion, infidelity; dishonesty, breach of trust, unfair dealing; falseness, perfidiousness; — be-īmānī karnā (-se), To act dishonestly, faithlessly, &c.; to play (one) false, to cheat, defraud, misappropriate
adj. & adv. Without reason, causeless, groundless; causelessly, &c.
adj. & adv. Without remainder or arrears, complete, paid up in full, balanced, even; entirely, completely, fully
v.t. To pay up in full, to complete; to wipe off, adjust, settle, liquidate, discharge (an account, &c.); to balance (accounts); to make good a default or balance
v.n. To be paid off, wiped off, settled, liquidated, &c.
s.f. Completion; payment in full, adjustment, discharge, liquidation, acquittance, clearance
v.t. To pay up in full, &c. ( = be-bāq karnā, q.v.)
adj. Unfledged, callow; — without resources, without power, powerless, impotent, helpless
adj. Unconsidered; — be-bićāre, adv. Without considering, inconsiderately, thoughtlessly
adj. Without change, invariable, uniform; unalterable, immutable; incomparable, inestimable, matchless, peerless
adj. Without power, authority, or command; without resource, powerless, helpless, weak; unresisting
adj. Unenduring, not eternal, perishable, frail
adj. Without power, weak; destitute, poor, wretched
adj. Unarranged, without order, unsettled (as a country
adj. Devoid of foundation, groundless, unfounded
adj. Beyond price, priceless, invaluable ( = be-qīmat)
adj. Having no share, part or lot (in), without portion or profit; destitute, unfortunate
adj. lit. 'Without feet and head'; utterly without resources, very poor and wretched
adj. & adv. Unveiled, uncovered; immodest; openly
adj. Wanting in authority, unauthorized, &c. ( = nishpramāṇ, q.v.)
adj. & adv. Heedless, careless, unconcerned, without reflection, thoughtless; fearless, intrepid; at ease, independent; fearlessly, boldly, &c.
(-se), To be careless (of), to be indifferent (to); to be independent (of)
s.f. Freedom from danger or apprehension, security, tranquillity; carelessness, unconcern, thoughtlessness, inattention, heedlessness; independence; indifference
s.f. lit. 'The being without wings'; powerlessness, helplessness
adj. Having no spiritual guide; vicious
adj. Without sympathy or feeling, unfeeling, pitiless, cruel, merciless; inexorable
adj. Faint, powerless; agitated, restless, uneasy impatient ( = be-ćain); devoid of splendour, lustreless
adv. & adj. Restlessly, uneasy, impatient, impatiently; faint
s.f. Faintness; agitation, restlessness, uneasiness, impatience; lack of splendour or lustre
be-tāla, adj. Out of time (in music); ill-timed
adj. & adv. Without deliberation; without hesitation; extemporary; inconsiderate, rash; — unhesitatingly, ex tempore; inconsiderately
adj. & adv. Inconsiderate, without distinction; rash, reckless; rashly, recklessly, headlong
s.f. Freedom from connection or concern; state of separation or detachment, independence; solitariness
adj. & adv. Faultless, blameless, innocent; faultlessly, &c.
s.f. The being without ceremony, unceremoniousness, frankness
adj. Free from desire or longing, content
adj. Lacking discrimination, indiscreet, unwise, silly
s.m. Escheat, property devolving on the state through want of legal claimants: be-tauba, s.m. Impenitence
adj. Not to be depended on; uncertain; groundless
adj. & adv. Out of place; groundless; inconsistent; — groundlessly; inconsistently; improperly

adj. & adv. Out of place, ill-placed, misplaced, ill-timed; unbecoming, improper, amiss, unlawful, unjustifiable; unreasonable, absurd; foreign to the purpose, irrelevant; inaccurate, wrong, objectionable; — improperly, inopportunely; injudiciously, wrongly
bejā ṣarf, s.m. Improper or needless expenditure, needless expense, extravagance, waste
adj. Lifeless, inanimate, faint, dead; valiant, brave
v.t. To deprive of life, to slay
s.f. Want of courage, cowardliness, pusillanimity
Without blemish, without flaw, spotless (a jewel)
s.f. Want of beauty, ugliness; imperfection
adj. Without answer; unable to answer ( = lā-jawāb)
s.f. The being unable to answer; the being silenced
adj. & s.m. Uncultivated, untilled; — a farm that has been abandoned by the cultivators
adj. Without join or joint, out of joint, disjointed ( = ajoṛ)
adj. Untimely, out of season, unseasonable, out of occasion

adj. & s.m. (Pers. pl. be-ćāragān), Without remedy, means, or resources; without choice; helpless, destitute, unfortutunate, miserable, wretched; — unfortunate person, helpless person, poor fellow, poor wretch
adj. Lamp-less, dark, desolate, ruined beyond hope; childless
adj. Eyeless: be-ćoba, s.m. lit. 'Poleless'; a kind of tent pitched without a pole
adj. Out of condition, ill-circumstanced, indisposed; damaged, unserviceable, worn out, used up, ruined; unemployed, badly off, in a bad way or state
s.f. Badness of circumstances or condition
be-ḥijābāna, adj. & adv. Unveiled, immodest; openly; shamelessly
s.f. Appearing unveiled, immodesty, indecency, shamelessness
be-ḥad, adj. Boundless, unbounded, endless ( = be-intihā)
s.f. Want of motion, motionlessness, immovability
adj. Countless, beyond calculation, incalculable; immoderate, excessive; inconsistent
be-ḥawāṣ, Out of one's senses, beside one's self, distracted
s.f. Senselessness, insensibility; distraction of mind
adj. Lacking ambition, mean or low-spirited, spiritless, wanting capacity
adj. & s.m. Shameless, immodest, bold, impudent; — shameless person, &c.
adj. Without thorns; without anxiety, solicitude, or fear
s.m. lit. 'Devoid of testicles'; an eunuch; — be-ḵẖāya karnā, v.t. To geld, castrate
adj. & adv. Without knowledge (of, -se), uninformed; without intelligence, senseless, ignorant, stupid; incautious, imprudent, careless, heedless; — unwittingly, unintentionally
adj. Without money for expenses, wanting means
s.f. Want of pay or wages, want of money
adj. Without a master, without an owner
be-ḵẖat̤ra, adj. Free from danger, safe, fearless, unapprehensive
adj. Without inclination or desire; without spirit; without pursuit, object, or hobby
be-ḵẖud, adj. Beside oneself (with joy or grief), out of one's mind; in ecstasy, transported, enraptured, intoxicated; senseless, delirious (syn. az ḵẖẉud rafta)
adv. Like one beside himself; like one in ecstasies, &c.
s.f. The being beside one's self, alienation of mind, ecstasy, transport, rapture; senselessness, insensibility, stupefaction, delirium
adj. Without food and sleep; without inclination to eat or sleep, restless, unsettled, disturbed
adj. Not admitted, set aside, dismissed (a claim)
s.f. Injustice; iniquity; — adj. Not doing justice, unjust, lawless (see be-inṣāf; be-inṣāfī)
s.f. Carelessness, inattention, neglect (particularly of cattle)
adj. & s.m. Grainless; seedless (as fruit); — pomegranate; a kind of raisin; the fruit of the mulberry tree
adj. & s.m. Dispossessed, ejected; — dispossessed or ejected person; — be-daḵẖl karnā, v.t. To exclude, eject, evict
s.f. Want of entrance, exclusion, want of possession; dispossession
adj. Unfeeling, void of compassion, pitiless, merciless
adv. Like an unfeeling or merciless person; in an unfeeling manner, &c.
s.f. Freedom from pain; unfeelingness, inhumanity
adj. & adv. Undeniable, incontestable, readily acknowledged; unsparing, ungrudging, not refusing, liberal; pitiless, merciless; — ungrudgingly, &c.
adj. lit. 'Without hands and feet'; without power or authority; without resources; helpless ( = be-pā-o-sar)
adj. Without claim; free from claims; — be-daʻwā-patra, s.m. A deed acknowledging the abandonment of a claim or suit
adj. Dissatisfied, displeased; heartless, dispirited, dejected, sad
adv. Like one heartless or dejected; in a spiritless or heartless manner; — be-dil honā (-se), To be dispirited or disheartened (by reason of); to be discontented or dissatisfied (with)
adj. Ill-tempered, irritable, impatient, easily provoked
adj. Beyond cure or remedy, incurable
be-dhaṛkā, adj. & adv. Without fear or doubt, fearless, bold, intrepid, dauntless; — fearlessly, &c.; confidently; on a sudden
adj. Without feeling or pity, unfeeling, pitiless
adj. & s.m. Without religion, irreligious; — irreligious person
adj. & adv. Fearless, bold, dauntless, intrepid; — fearlessly, &c.
adj. Misshapen, shapeless, ill-fashioned, ugly, clumsy, awkward; ill-mannered, ill-bred, uneducated; unpleasant, untoward (cf. be-ḍhab)
s.f. Ugliness; clumsiness, awkwardness; impropriety; disorder

adj. & adv. Ill-shaped, ugly, ungainly; unmannerly, rude, &c. ( = be-ḍaul); awkward, untoward, difficult, unmanageable; complicated, serious, grave, threatening, dangerous; severe, violent; ungovernable, daring, fearless, reckless, audacious, presumptuous; excessive, extravagant, enormous; strange, extraordinary; heartless, unfeeling, cruel; — fearlessly, daringly, recklessly; doggedly, impudently; excessively; severely, seriously; awkwardly, untowardly; cleverly; strikingly, in an extraordinary manner; cruelly, unfeelingly
adj. Ill-mannered, ill-behaved, ill-bred, uneducated, improper, ugly (see be-ḍhab), — be-ḍhangī, s.f. Ill-manneredness, rudeness, uncouthness; impropriety
adj. Erring, astray; unprincipled, depraved, dissolute
s.f. Absence or lack of rank, or degree of honour
adj. Without dignity or rank; worthless
adj. lit. 'Without face'; bare-faced, shameless; inhuman
adj. Destitute of daily bread, without sustenance; unfortunate
be-rok-ṭok, adj. Without let or hindrance
be-riyā, adj. Without guile, guileless, candid, sincere
adj. Displeased, vexed, annoyed, out of humour; disgusted; (vulg.) sick, sorry
(-se), To be displeased (with); to be sick (of)
s.f. Displeasure, ill-humour, vexation, annoyance; disgust, weariness
adj. & s.m. Without speech or language, mute, speechless, dumb; — a dumb animal, brute
s.f. Inability to speak, speechlessness, dumbness
adj. Without money or the means of getting it, helpless, destitute
adj. (Taking or getting) without purchase, free of cost
s.f. The being without money, impecuniosity, indigence, poverty
adj. Without gall or bile; patient; good-tempered; forbearing; indefatigable; shameless
adj. Ugly and awkward; inelegant in dress and person
s.f. Artlessness, simplicity, undisguised or unstudied conduct
adj. & adv. Inartificial, unaffected, natural, undisguised, plain, artless; — spontaneously, of its own accord, naturally; ex tempore
be-sāz-o-sāmān, adj. Without apparatus, or tools, &c.; unaccoutred
s.f. Want of apparatus, or of necessaries
adj. & adv. Without cause or reason, causeless; causelessly
s.f. Unveiling, exposure, dishonour; barefacedness, impudence
adj. Without pillar or prop; of no foundation; insignificant
adj. Senseless, beside one's self; enraptured, entranced, in a swoon (syn. be-ḵẖẉud)
adj. Without apparatus; without means; destitute of (the) necessaries (of life), unprovided, helpless
adj. Devoid of understanding or sense, ignorant, stupid ( = an-samajh; abudh)
adj. Thoughtless, unreflecting, undiscriminating, inconsiderate; heedless, regardless, careless
adj. & s.m. Ignorant, uninformed; — stupid fellow, blockhead
vulg. be-shafqat, adj. Unkind, unmerciful, harsh
be-shakk-o-raib, be-shakk-o-shubh, adv. Doubtless, indubitably, undoubtedly, certainly
adj. Devoid of patience, impatient, restless
v.n. To be impatient, to lose all patience
be-ṣabūrī, s.f. Want of patience, impatience
adj. Without the advice (of, -ke); ill-advised, head-strong
adj. Irregular, contrary to rule ( = ḵẖilaf-ẓābit̤a)
adj. Without rule or order, devoid of order or connection
adj. Powerless, weak, beyond the power of endurance
adj. Destitute of good fortune, unlucky
adj. & adv. Ill-mannered, unmannerly, uncivil, rude, awkward, uncouth; — badly, &c. ( = be-ḍhab, q.v.)
adj. Of no party, impartial, unsectarian
adj. Free from covetousness or greediness; disinterested
adj. Unmannerly, rude, &c. ( = be-t̤araḥ and be-ḍhab, qq.v.)
adj. Without honour or dignity; disgraced
s.f. Ingloriousness, disesteem, dishonour, disgrace, ignominy
adj. Without defect, blemish, or flaw; faultless
adj. Disinterested, without selfishness, impartial; independent, indifferent
s.f. Disinterestedness, unselfishness; impartiality, indifference, independence
be-g̠al-o-g̠ash, adj. & adv. Without trouble or anxiety; without vexation, annoyance, or any disturbing influence; without fraud or hatred
adj. & adv. Without trouble, without sorrow or anxiety
adj. & adv. Without consideration or reflection, without premeditation; — thoughtlessly, &c.
adj. Lacking honour or dignity; without pride or emulation, spiritless; shameless, wanton, impudent, rude, infamous
s.f. Want of honour, &c.; want of pride, &c.; spiritlessness; disgrace; shamelessness, impudence, rudeness, wantonness
adj. & adv. Unprofitable, useless, vain; uselessly, to no purpose
adj. Free from care or anxiety, without solicitude, contented, tranquil; unconcerned, thoughtless, unreflecting, inconsiderate
s.f. Freedom from care, anxiety or solicitude, contentedness; thoughtlessness, unconcern
adj. Unprofitable, possessing but not bestowing (as a learned man who does not communicate his knowledge, or a rich man from whose wealth no one derives benefit)
adj. Unable, powerless, helpless; without restraint; out of one's power or reach, secure against surprise or attack
adj. Unarranged, irregular, without order or rule, ungrammatical
adj. Of no importance or worth, of no estimation, unimportant, worthless
s.f. Worthlessness, low estimation, disesteem, disrepute

adj. Restless, uneasy, discomposed, disturbed in mind, disquieted, anxious, distracted; unsettled, variable, vacillating, inconstant; — be-qarār karnā, v.t. To render uneasy, disturb in mind, disquiet, &c.
s.f. Restlessness, uneasiness, anxiety, discomposure, disquietude; instability, inconstancy, variableness, fluctuation
adj. Faultless, innocent; without fail, completely, entirely
adj. Inconceivable, contrary to the common order of things, incomprehensible, immense, exorbitaut
adj. Unrestrained, unrestricted; — irregular; dissolute, licentious
be-kār, be-kām, adj. Without work or employment, unemployed, not in office, idle; inoperative, ineffective; without force, invalid; useless, worthless
v.t. To render useless or unserviceable; to render of no avail, to render defective; to nullify, invalidate; to thwart, frustrate, defeat, undo
s.f. The state of being unemployed; want of employment, idleness
adj. Disappointed, frustrated: be-kuṭnāye, adv. Without practising the work, business, or art of a procuress
adj. Friendless, forlorn, destitute; — be-kas-āna, adj. & adv. Friendless, destitute; in a friendless manner
s.f. The being buried without a shroud
adj. Restless, uneasy, disturbed ( = be-ćain; be-qarār); out of order
adj. Without decrease or diminution, without omission, accurate, exact, all right
adj. Free from anxiety or apprehension
adj. Without doubt or suspicion; doubtless
adj. & adv. Guiltless, innocent; — without offence, unjustly, wrongfully
be-gintī, adj. Countless, numberless, &c. ( = be-shumār; an-gina)
adj. Inaccessible (a river, &c.), without steps or a quay (to it)
adj. Without stain or blemish, faultless, irreproachable
adj. Inattentive, unmindful, indiscreet; unmannerly, impertinent
adj. Unkind, ungracious; inelegant; without pleasure, insipid, flavourless, vapid: be-lut̤fī, s.f. Unkindness; inelegance; unpleasantness
adj. Unconnected, independent; without ties or encumbrances, unfettered; inaccessible; candid; categorical, without reserve
adj. Without resources, without means, funds, or substance; poor, destitute, indigent
adj. & adv. Without respect, unceremonious; unceremoniously
adj. & adv. Unaccountable, irresponsible; without calculation, at a venture
adj. & adv. Out of place, improper; improperly
adj. Unkind, inhuman, cruel; unpolished, unpolite, uncivil
s.f. Unkindness, inhumanity, cruelty; unpoliteness, incivility
s.f. Insipidity, tastelessness; coolness (between friends); unpleasantness; disgust
adj. Unmeaning, senseless, absurd, foolish, idle, vain
adj. Without authority; without resource, poor, miserable

adj. Independent, unwilling to incur obligation: be-minnatā, s.m. Name of a bicornous instrument made of wood with a cloth spread over it for straining bhaṅg (so called because a man can strain the liquor without help from others)
adj. & adv. Without cause or reason, causeless; causelessly
adj. & adv. Out of season, unseasonable; unseasonably
be-mauqiʻ, adj. & adv. Out of place; unseasonable, untimely, unapt, inopportune, inconvenient
adj. Wanting in affection, unkind, unfriendly
s.f. Want of affection, unkindness, unfriendliness
adj. Without unison, unharmonious, unsuitable, unreconciled, &c. ( = an-mel, q.v.)
s.m. A lease guaranteeing that the average rates of land remain as before
adj. Without name, character, or reputation; inglorious
adj. Without lot or portion (in, -se), unfortunate (in respect of, -se); frustrated (in)
s.f. Untoward luck or fortune, ill-luck, misfortune
adj. Without salt, saltless; insipid; lacking piquancy; ugly; ordinary
adj. Shameless; — be-nang-o-nāmus, adj. Without name or character
adj. & s.m. Without provisions or furniture; without prosperity or splendour in condition; indigent, destitute; — a kind of darwesh who shaves his eyebrows and beard (syn. āzād)
adj. Without bounds or limits, boundless, endless
adj. Without want, wanting nothing, not in need, able to dispense (with, -se), independent (of); an epithet of the Deity
v.t. To make (one) independent (of, -se); to make (a thing) unnecessary (for one)
s.f. Freedom from want, ability to dispense (with), independence
adj. Without restraint, without restriction, unrestricted; without rule
s.m. Property that escheats to the government in default of heirs
adj. Without a ruler, without a protector
adj. & adv. Without cause or reason; causelessly
adj. Insolent, rude, unmannerly; unmindful; immodest, shameless
adj. & s.m. Without home or native country; — an exile
adj. & s.m. Faithless, perfidious, treacherous; ungrateful; — traitor, faithless one, ingrate
adj. Without fame or character, wanting dignity, undignified; dishonourable
adj. Out of season, untimely, ill-timed, at an unusual time, unseasonable
adj. & s.m. Ignorant, foolish, inexpert, stupid; — blockhead, dolt
s.f. Want of understanding, ignorance, stupidity, folly, foolishness, fatuity: be-hamāl, adj. Without compeer, unequalled, unrivalled ( = be-naz̤īr; be-badal)
adj. Unambitious, unaspiring, humble, spiritless, pusillanimous; miserly, ignoble, vile; slothful, lazy, indolent
s.f. Lack of ambition, spiritlessness; pusillanimity, &c. (see be-himmat)
adj. Unconscious, insensible, stupefied, intoxicated, delirious; senseless, stupid
s.f. unconsciousness, insensibility, stupefaction, intoxication, delirium; senselessness, stupidity
adv. For nothing, without reason, purpose, or motive.

def: particle (masc.) used contemptuously (see abe)
subs: (se), To address rudely or contemptuously (see abe-tabe).

r: be, ba, Urdū, Hindūstānī, Nāgarī, b, abjad, rajab, va, wa, v, w, aber, aver, seb, sew, m and p, baur, maul, narbadā, narmadā, bādshāh, pādshāh, ba-kār
ety: and of the Persian and Arabic
def: s.m. The letter or sound b.
s.f. Name of a grass (from which a twine is made; it is also used in thatching).
s.m. (f. -ī), Seller, one who offers for sale ( = bećū); — selling (used in comp.)
subs: s.f. Buying and selling, trade, commerce

def: v.t. To sell, dispose of; to endorse (a cheque or bill)
subs: s.m. Exclusive right of selling, monopoly

def: s.m. Seller ( = bećā).
s.f. Willow; cane, ratan, Calamus rotang
subs: s.m. One who works on ratans, basket-maker; — bed-bāfī, s.f. Weaving with ratans (as the bottoms of chairs, and the panels of doors or windows)

def: s.m. The sacred scriptures of the Hindūs; — (The original Veda is believed to have been revealed by Brahmā and to have been preserved by tradition until it was arranged in its present order by a certain sage who thence obtained the surname of Vyās or Vedavyās, i.e. compiler of the Vedas
subs: He distributed the sacred writings into four parts
viz. 1˚, the Rig-veda; 2˚ the Yajush or Yajur-Veda; 3˚, the Sāma-Veda; 4˚, the Atharva-Veda); — divine knowledge; faith, certainty

(veda+anga), s.m. The general term for the six auxiliary or subordinate branches of the Vedas, viz. (1), Śikshā, or the rules of pronunciation and recital; (2), Kalpa, or the details of religious ceremonies; (3), Vyākaraṇ, or grammar; (4), Ćhand, or prosody; (5), Nirukti, or the explanation of difficult, obscure, or obsolete words that occur in the Vedas; (6), Jyotis or astronomy.

def: s.m. = H بيدنا बेदना bedanā [S. वेदना], s.f. Perception, knowledge, knowledge conveyed by the senses; pain, smart, ache, agony, torment, affliction, sickness, distress.
s.f. = H بيدن बेदन bedan [S. वेदन], s.m. Perception, knowledge, knowledge conveyed by the senses; pain, smart, ache, agony, torment, affliction, sickness, distress.
s.m. The entire or complete Veda; name of a particular Hindū system of philosophy and theology.
s.m. One who is conversant in the Vedānta system; follower of the Vedānta philosophy.
adj. Awake, wakeful, sleepless; watching, watchful, vigilant, alert
subs: adj. Watchful, alert; quick of apprehension; — bedār-dilī, s.f. Watchfulness, alertness, quickness of apprehension
v.n. To awake, rouse up, be alert, &c.

def: s.f. Waking, wakefulness; vigilance, watchfulness.
s.m. The castor beaver.
s.m. Piercing, penetrating, breaking through; perforation, excavation, hole, bore, crack; depth.
adj. & s.m. Piercing, perforating; sharp; — piercer, borer; wounder, stabber.
s.m. Piercing, &c. (see next).
s.m. Piercer, borer, &c. = bedhak, q.v.
s.m. Piercer, borer, &c. = bedhak, q.v.
v.t. To pierce, por forate, bore; to wound, stab.
s.f. = bedanā, q.v.

s.f. Altar or raised place (made of wood and kuśa grass and prepared for an oblation for placing the vessels used at a sacrifice); place or ground prepared for sacrifice, altar; platform, bench (syn. bedikā).
s.m. A learned Brāhmaṇ; scholar, teacher.
s.f. Altar; platform, ( = bedī).
s.m. Reader of the Vedas.
s.m. & adv. Speed, celerity, haste, rapidity; violence, impetuosity; — dung; — with haste, speedily, quickly, soon.

r: beg, imāla, bag, baga, bagha, bhaga
ety: by imāla for bag; Old P. baga; Zend bagha; S. bhaga
def: s.m. Lord, master; a Mog̠al title corresponding with the English Lord.
s.f. Lady; queen: a title of Mogal ladies.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Strange, foreign, another, not related, not domestic, not an acquaintance or friend, alien, unknown; — stranger, foreigner
subs: adj. Of an unfriendly, or savage disposition

def: s.f. Strangeness, the being foreign or not domestic; estrangement; shyness.
s.m. Compelling to work for nothing; compulsory labour with or without pay; impressment of workmen, carriage, &c.; — a person forced to labour ( = be-gārī)
subs: v.t. To press into service with or without pay
v.n. To work in a half-hearted or perfunctory manner; to work carelessly.

def: s.m. Gold tinsel; tin-foil; varnish.
s.m. A person pressed into service, forced labourer, one pressed to carry burdens for individuals or the public; — s.f. The act of pressing or forcing to work
subs: s.m. One compelled to work, a pressed man
v.t. To take forcibly (with or without pay), to press.
